hero.h1a: Schools do not need more software.
hero.sub: Sapiion is not built to add features to the pile. It is built to replace the pile.

pattern.label: The pattern
pattern.h2: Schools invest heavily in improvement. Yet the results rarely match the ambition.
pattern.body: This is not because educators lack dedication. It is because the tools they work with cannot sustain the change. The same cycle repeats, year after year.
steps: A problem is identified — student results, outcome coverage, accreditation readiness.
External consultants are brought in. Training is delivered. The ideas are excellent.
Teachers are enthusiastic. New action plans and agreements are made.
Everyone returns to daily work. Teaching, grading, administration.
There is no system to reinforce the new way of working. Practices gradually drift back.
Two years later, the same problems are discussed in the same meetings.
pattern.quoteA: "Educational quality doesn't improve because of good ideas alone.
pattern.quoteB: It improves when good ideas become part of everyday practice."
pattern.quoteSub: Without a connected platform, continuous improvement depends on individual effort. With a connected platform, continuous improvement becomes part of the institution's way of working.

realGap.label: The real problem
realGap.h2: The problem is not ambition. Most institutions already have all the right intentions.
realGap.body: The gap is between educational ambition and everyday practice. Schools have vision, investment and dedicated people — but lack a platform that turns those intentions into daily work.

missingItems.label: A connected platform that turns ambition into daily practice
missingItems.body: Without a system that embeds new ways of working into everyday teaching, improvement fades — and the same problems resurface two years later.

missingItems.label: Connected data from curriculum to quality assurance
missingItems.body: When data lives in separate systems, no one has the full picture. Signals are missed, decisions are reactive, and evidence has to be assembled from scratch.

realGap.conclusion: Sapiion exists to turn educational ambition into everyday practice.

whatSapiionIs.label: What Sapiion really is
whatSapiionIs.h2: Sapiion is an Education Intelligence Platform — and the implementation layer for educational improvement.
whatSapiionIs.body1: Schools can improve without Sapiion — but it is much harder to improve consistently, at scale, and over time. Sapiion does not replace educator judgement. It gives that judgement a system to work within.
whatSapiionIs.body2: It connects the people, processes and information that have traditionally lived in separate systems — into one continuous workflow where improvement is no longer an annual project. It becomes part of everyday teaching.
whatSapiionIs.connectedBy: ◆ All connected by Sapiion — not as a sequence, but as a closed loop. Every stage generates evidence. That evidence feeds the next stage — and ultimately feeds back into curriculum improvement.
whatSapiionIs.connectedBy2: Workplace learning does not end when the student returns to campus. Every placement generates evidence about employer expectations, emerging competencies and skills gaps, feeding directly into future curriculum improvement.
whatSapiionIs.employersNote: Sapiion connects not only the institution, but also workplace supervisors and industry partners — allowing workplace learning to become an integrated part of educational quality.

whereFits.label: Where Sapiion fits
whereFits.body: Sapiion is the intelligence layer that connects every stage of the educational process — from the moment curriculum is designed to the moment a quality review is conducted.

comparison.h2: What most institutions have today — and what Sapiion provides.
rows: Curriculum in a live, connected system
Grading connected to outcomes and rubrics
Internships connected to curriculum, supervision, assessment and student success

vsLms.h2: Sapiion is not a Learning Management System (LMS).
vsLms.body: Traditional LMS platforms like Canvas, Moodle and Brightspace primarily focus on content delivery and course administration. They do that well. But they were not designed to connect curriculum intent, teaching practice, assessment evidence and institutional quality into one continuous improvement cycle. That is a different problem — and that is what Sapiion solves.
vsLms.sapiionQ: "Did the student develop the intended competency — and how do we know?"
vsLms.footer: That question changes everything about how a platform is built. An LMS does Content → Assignment → Grade. Sapiion runs a closed improvement loop: Curriculum Design → Teaching → Assessment → Student Success → Workplace Learning → Educational Intelligence → Curriculum Improvement → (repeat). Every stage generates evidence. That evidence feeds the next stage. The loop never stops. No LMS, SIS or standalone internship platform can claim that — and that means institutions can answer questions that were never possible before: Which competencies are employers requesting more often? Which modules need updating? Which skills gaps appear consistently across companies?

vsSis.h2: Sapiion is not a student administration system.
vsSis.body1: Osiris, Educator and similar SIS platforms manage student records — enrolments, credits, diplomas. They record what happened. Sapiion helps improve what happens.
vsSis.body2: A SIS records the past. Sapiion helps improve the future.
vsSis.body3: A SIS tells you a student failed. Sapiion identifies risk early, giving educators and programme leaders the insight they need to intervene before it's too late.

ai.h2: AI built on educational context — not generic content.
ai.body1: Artificial intelligence is becoming part of education. The question is not whether AI will be used, but how it will be used responsibly.
ai.mostAiQ: "Start without knowledge of your specific educational context."
ai.sapiionQ: "Applies AI within your curriculum, your outcomes, your assessment criteria, your students' progress."
ai.body2: Because Sapiion already understands your curriculum, learning outcomes, assessments, workplace learning and student progress, AI can support meaningful educational decisions — instead of generating disconnected content.
studentItems: Receive guidance that supports learning instead of replacing thinking
ai.principle: Sapiion does not use AI to replace teachers. It uses AI to strengthen the intelligence already present in education.

accreditation.body: Whether the review is an accreditation visit, an inspection or an internal audit — the stress is always the same: evidence is scattered across systems that were never designed to work together. With Sapiion, that evidence is collected continuously, not assembled in a panic.
points: Every learning outcome linked to curriculum intent
Every assessment linked to rubric criteria
Every rubric score linked to outcomes
Every student's progress tracked against programme requirements
Every programme's health visible in real time
accreditation.footer: When the review comes, you present evidence — you do not collect it.

implementation.h2: Your first 90 days with Sapiion — not a 2-year implementation project.
implementation.body: Your programme does not stop while you implement Sapiion. It improves while you do.

steps.text: Institution setup, user accounts, basic curriculum structure
First assessments and grading workflows live
Coordinator dashboards active, student progress visible

gdpr.h2: Your data belongs to your institution. Full stop.
gdpr.sub: Everything listed here is available today.

items.title: EU hosted
items.body: Your data stays in Europe. Always.

items.title: Full data isolation
items.body: Each institution's data is completely separate — no data mixing.

items.title: GDPR — DPA ready
items.body: Data Processing Agreement available before any contract is signed.

items.title: No lock-in
items.body: Full data export at any time. Your data is yours.

items.title: Role-based access control
items.body: Administrators, coordinators, teachers and students each see only what they need.

items.title: Audit logging
items.body: All critical actions are logged — who did what and when.

gdpr.roadmapSub: We are actively building the integrations that institutions expect for enterprise deployment.

internshipCallout.body1: Traditional systems treat internships as an administrative requirement.
internshipCallout.body2: Sapiion treats every internship as educational evidence.
internshipCallout.footer: Together they create institutional knowledge that helps curriculum teams keep education aligned with the changing labour market.

livingSystem.body: Sapiion continuously connects classroom evidence, workplace learning, student development and programme intelligence — enabling institutions to improve not only how they teach, but also what they teach.

cta.h2: Ready to see what connected education looks like?
